learningObjectives.title: Understand SAP TM objects
learningObjectives.description: Identify key business objects used in SAP TM lifecycle.

learningObjectives.title: Differentiate FU and FO
learningObjectives.description: Explain planning vs execution roles in transportation documents.

learningObjectives.title: Recognize TM master data
learningObjectives.description: Understand Business Partner, Location, Resource and related data.

sections.content: SAP Transportation Management introduces several transportation-specific business objects used during planning, execution and settlement. Understanding these objects is essential before learning transportation planning.

sections.title: Remember
sections.content: Every transportation process in SAP TM revolves around Transportation Orders (TORs). Freight Units, Freight Orders and Freight Bookings are different types of Transportation Orders.

sections.title: SAP TM Objects
rows: Transportation demand generated from business documents
Represents ocean or air carrier booking

sections.content: A Freight Unit represents transportation demand. It is automatically generated from business documents such as Outbound Deliveries or Purchase Orders and serves as the primary planning object in SAP TM.
A Freight Order is an executable transportation document created after planning. It contains transportation stages, assigned resources, carriers and execution information.
Freight Booking is primarily used for ocean and air transportation where transportation capacity is reserved with external carriers.

A customer order creates an outbound delivery. SAP TM generates a Freight Unit from the delivery. Multiple Freight Units are consolidated into one Freight Order for execution by an assigned carrier. After delivery, freight costs are settled through a Freight Settlement Document.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: An automotive manufacturer generates approximately 1,200 Freight Units daily. SAP TM consolidates them into 180 Freight Orders, reducing transportation cost and improving truck utilization.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: One of the most frequently asked SAP TM interview questions is 'What is the difference between Freight Unit and Freight Order?' Be able to explain both from business and technical perspectives.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Do not say Freight Unit is used for transportation execution. Freight Units are planning objects, while Freight Orders are execution documents.

items: Transportation Order is the generic TM document.
Freight Booking is used for ocean and air transportation.
Business Partner, Location and Resource are important TM master data.
